title: Daily Market Digest: April 4, 2026
subtitle: Market closes for Good Friday amid Middle East tensions and strong jobs report.

items.headline: US Markets Closed for Good Friday; Futures Point Lower as Middle East Tensions Persist
items.summary: U.S. stock markets are closed today for the Good Friday holiday. Futures on the three major US stock indexes were down around 0.2% as investors digested a stronger-than-expected jobs report and continued to assess developments in the Middle East, including intensified rhetoric from President Trump against Iran. Despite recent weekly gains, caution prevails given geopolitical uncertainty and elevated energy prices.

items.headline: Franklin Covey Co. leads April gains
items.summary: Franklin Covey Co. is noted as the best-performing stock in April 2026, with a significant monthly gain.

items.headline: Eastman Kodak sees strong April rise
items.summary: Eastman Kodak Co. has posted a strong gain in the initial days of April.

items.headline: Spire Global continues upward trend
items.summary: Spire Global, Inc. is among the top performers for April 2026.

items.headline: Lipocine Inc. faces sharp decline
items.summary: Lipocine Inc. experienced the biggest decline in early April, following topline safety and efficacy results for LPCN 1154 in patients with postpartum depression.

items.headline: Pasithea Therapeutics Warrants drop
items.summary: Pasithea Therapeutics Corp. Warrants saw a significant drop after announcing the grant of Fast Track Designation by FDA to PAS-004 for Neurofibromatosis Type 1.

items.headline: Inovio Pharmaceuticals affected by public offering news
items.summary: Inovio Pharmaceuticals, Inc. declined significantly following an announced proposed public offering.

items.headline: US Economy Adds 178,000 Jobs in March; Unemployment Dips to 4.3%
items.summary: American employers added a robust 178,000 new jobs last month, rebounding strongly from a dismal February. The unemployment rate also dipped to 4.3%. Health care companies led job growth, adding 76,400 positions. Average hourly wages rose 0.2% from February and 3.5% year-over-year, aligning with the Federal Reserve's 2% annual inflation target.

items.headline: Federal Reserve Issues Prohibition Against Former United Bank Employee
items.summary: The Federal Reserve Board announced an enforcement action on April 4, 2026, issuing a consent prohibition against Mollie Morrow, a former employee of United Bank in Fairfax, Virginia, for embezzling bank funds. This action underscores the Fed's commitment to maintaining the integrity of the banking system.

items.headline: FOMC Expected to Maintain Interest Rates in April Meeting
items.summary: Prediction markets indicate a high probability (97.7%) that the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) will not change interest rates after its meeting scheduled for April 28-29, 2026.

items.headline: Technology and Communication Services Led Weekly Gains; Energy Lagged
items.summary: For the week ending April 2, 2026, technology stocks surged 4.61%, and communication services rose 4.36%, making them the best-performing sectors. Conversely, the energy sector fell 5.1%, and consumer defensives saw only a 0.72% gain, making them the worst-performing sectors.

items.headline: Middle East Tensions Continue to Drive Oil Prices Higher
items.summary: Ongoing geopolitical developments in the Middle East, including the war with Iran, continue to impact global markets. Brent crude oil prices are above $116 a barrel, with the average price for a gallon of gas in the United States reaching $4, contributing to concerns about inflation.

items.headline: Union County Business Park Poised for Multiple Data Centers
items.summary: Plans were revealed for up to four data centers to be developed in a business park in Union County, Pennsylvania, by PNK Group of New York City. This development aims to meet the growing market demand for data center capacity in the Mid-Atlantic Region.
